title: LEL Event-Match Records Schema
description: JSON Schema (draft-07) for 06_LEARNING_LAYER/OBSERVATIONS/lel_event_match_records.json. One record per LEL event. Cited at M4-A-T3-RUBRIC-SCHEMA (NAP.M4.1). Consumed by LL.1 Signal Weight Calibration (MACRO_PLAN_v2_0.md §LL-Appendix.B LL.1) and by signal_weights/ writes during M4-B. Required fields per PHASE_M4_PLAN_v1_0.md §3.1 AC.M4A.2. v1.1 adds rubric_option (outer + per-record), total_events, held_out_count, training_count, held_out_manifest at outer level (M4-A-INTEGRATION-PASS-R3).

schema_version.description: Version of this schema document (frozen). Bumped when fields change.

rubric_version.description: Version of CALIBRATION_RUBRIC_v1_0.md frozen at the time these records were scored. Required so a future re-scoring under a new rubric is comparable to the original.

rubric_option.description: Which rubric option (A/B/C from CALIBRATION_RUBRIC_v1_0.md) was approved by the native and used for all records in this file. Matches the per-record rubric_option field. Added v1.1 (M4-A-INTEGRATION-PASS-R3) after NAP.M4.1 approved Option B.

produced_during.description: Cowork or Claude Code session ID that produced this records file.

produced_on.description: Date the records file was authored (YYYY-MM-DD).

total_events.description: Total number of LEL events covered (training + held_out). Must equal len(records). At LEL v1.5: 46.

held_out_count.description: Number of held-out events. These records carry partition: held_out and are NEVER consumed by LL.1 writes per AC.M4A.4.

training_count.description: Number of training events (total_events - held_out_count). These records carry partition: training.

held_out_manifest.description: Summary of the held-out partition selection. Decade-stratified per AC.M4A.4 (≥20% of total events). Authoritative list of held-out event IDs.

records.description: One element per LEL event. Length must equal total_events (46 events at LEL v1.5).

event_match_record.description: A single per-event record. Cites the LEL event by event_id, the rubric version used to score, the resolved event date, and the per-signal expected/actual stacks with lit_scores per the rubric.

event_id.description: LEL event ID. Must match an EVT.* row in 01_FACTS_LAYER/LIFE_EVENT_LOG_v1_2.md.

event_date_used.description: The concrete YYYY-MM-DD date used for proximity scoring. Resolution rule for fuzzy LEL dates is specified in CALIBRATION_RUBRIC_v1_0.md §3 (mid-month for month-exact; July 1 for approx_year; per-event override for proxy).

event_date_confidence.description: Confidence level of the LEL event date as carried into this record. Determines which date-resolution rule was applied to event_date_used.

rubric_version.description: Version of CALIBRATION_RUBRIC_v1_0.md under which this specific record was scored. Should match the parent file's rubric_version unless this is a per-record override (rare; logged in match_notes if so).

rubric_option.description: Which rubric option (A/B/C) was used to score this specific record. In the v1.1 file all records carry 'B' (NAP.M4.1 approved). Per-record field exists to support future mixed-option re-scoring in a single file. Added v1.1 (M4-A-INTEGRATION-PASS-R3).

partition.description: Which partition this event belongs to. Held-out events are NEVER consumed by LL.1 training writes per Learning Layer discipline #4 (PHASE_M4_PLAN AC.M4A.4). The held-out partition is decade-stratified, ≥9 events of the 46.

expected_lit_signals.description: The set of MSR signals that the LEL retrodictive_match block (or the temporal substrate's classical-rule expansion) names as expected to fire at this event. Each entry is the per-signal expected metadata.

signal_id.description: MSR signal ID (SIG.MSR.NNN, CVG.NN, CTR.NN, RPT.*, or other namespace per FORENSIC). Must resolve to a row in MSR_v3_0.md or be flagged as classical-rule expansion in basis.

signal_domain.description: Primary domain assignment for this signal under the rubric's domain-mapping table. Multi-domain signals carry their primary; alternates may be recorded in match_notes.

basis.description: Provenance of the expectation: classical_rule = drawn from MSR/CGM/UCN classical claim; temporal_engine = derived from M3 Vimshottari/Yogini/Chara/transit substrate; both = supported by both.

actual_lit_signals.description: The set of MSR signals that actually fired (lit) at the event date per the activator output, scored under the rubric. May include signals not in expected_lit_signals (surplus matches) and may omit signals from expected_lit_signals (misses).

signal_id.description: MSR signal ID; must resolve to MSR_v3_0.md.

lit_score.description: Numeric score in [0.0, 1.0] per the rubric's scoring scale. Under Option B: {0.0, 0.2, 0.5, 0.7, 1.0}. Under Option A: {0, 1}. Under Option C: typically inherits the per-event 1.0/0.5/0.0 single value.

lit_source.description: Which axis of activation produced the lit_score. Per CALIBRATION_RUBRIC §2.2, dasha and transit axes are scored separately and combined per the rubric's combined-aggregator rule (default: max).

match_rate.description: Per-event aggregate score per the rubric's aggregation rule. Under Option B: sum(lit_score for s in expected_lit_signals) / count(expected_lit_signals). Under Option C: single domain-match score. The downstream LL.1 input.

match_notes.description: Free-text notes on scoring decisions: per-signal partial-credit rationale, multi-domain assignments, per-event date-center overrides, surplus-match observations, activator quirks. Keep concise; this is the audit trail for the score.

computation_session.description: Cowork or Claude Code session ID under which this record was scored. Distinct from produced_during at the parent level when records are scored in batches across multiple sessions.
